heading: Authority to enter into contracts for private counsel
label: 4241 Authority to enter into contracts for private counsel

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Attorney General may enter into contracts retaining private counsel to furnish legal services, including representation in investigation, negotiation, compromise, settlement, litigation, and execution of judgments in the case of any civil action referred to in or .section 4201 of this titlesection 4225 of this title

children.heading: Terms and conditions
children.label: b Terms and conditions
children.text: Each contract under subsection (a) shall include the provisions described in and such other terms and conditions as the Attorney General considers necessary and appropriate to protect the interests of the United States.section 4244 of this title

children.heading: Limitation of fee
children.label: c Limitation of fee
children.text: The amount of the contingency fee payable for legal services furnished under a contract described in subsection (a) shall not exceed the contingency fee that counsel engaged in the private practice of law in the jurisdiction wherein the legal services are furnished typically charge clients for furnishing the same or comparable legal services.

children.heading: Contingent fees
children.label: d Contingent fees
children.text: Notwithstanding , a contract under this section shall provide that a fee that the United States pays private counsel for services is payable from the amount recovered and shall be based on a percentage of the civil penalties or assets recovered.section 3302(b) of title 31
